title: campanula garganica
intro: Campanula garganica, also known as the Carpathian harebell, is a perennial plant species native to the Carpathian Mountains in Eastern Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, colds, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Campanula garganica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make medicinal remedies, although it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal preparations.
